structured.title: Couple Argues About Future Church Marriage Course
structured.overview: The recording captures a tense argument between a couple, Rachel and her husband (likely a church leader), about running a marriage course at their church. Rachel explains that Chloe messaged to say she and Andrea are still interested in attending whenever it happens. Rachel has replied that she remains interested in making it happen and will think about scheduling it after mid‑May, keeping Chloe in the loop, but has not committed to specific dates. Her husband reacts strongly, warning that he feels she prematurely tells people about substantial initiatives like a marriage course before plans, leadership structure, and capacity (especially for couples needing more than video-based support) are properly thought through. He stresses that congregants may pin their hopes on such promises and can be disappointed if the course does not materialize. Rachel experiences his input as negative, lecturing, and disrespectful, pushes back that the decision is not solely his, and insists she has previously discussed the idea with him. She becomes frustrated with his communication style, states that she will not continue the conversation in that tone, and repeatedly asks him to leave the room, emphasizing that she wants to discuss it another time in a more respectful, collaborative way.

apps_results.content:
## Marriage course interest raised
- Chloe and Andrea message interest in marriage course  
- Prior casual mentions about running course later in year  
- Reply sent: will think after mid‑May, keep them updated  

## Disagreement about communication
- One accuses other of reacting negatively and lecturing  
- Dispute over what was actually promised about timing  
- Concern raised about telling people plans before they’re firm  

## Concerns about capacity and responsibility
- Many marriages in church said to be struggling  
- One stresses need for proper support, not just videos  
- Other notes no capacity for full counseling right now  

## Leadership and decision‑making tension
- Debate over involving elders and wider leadership team  
- One feels other “jumps ahead” and creates expectations  
- Other insists it’s not solely partner’s decision to make  

## Conflict escalation and shutdown
- Tone called disrespectful, draining, like being told off  
- One repeatedly ends conversation, asks other to leave room  
- Both reference “hard lessons” in communication and burnout  

## Next steps
- Plan: revisit course feasibility after mid‑May  
- Need calmer discussion including leadership on scope and support  
- Need agreement on how to communicate plans to others
